insights.headline: Mood movement, concern follow-up, and what the data keeps echoing
insights.lede: Across 2819 sessions, about 24% were concern-flagged. Estimates use only past sessions and the current session’s setup—nothing after the visit.

prediction_cards.label: Holdout separation score
prediction_cards.definition: Technical check on held-back residents: how well higher-risk sessions sort toward actual concern flags.

prediction_cards.label: Trend fit on held-back data
prediction_cards.hint: Typical gap on the mood scale: 0.603
prediction_cards.definition: How well a simple summary line matches mood change scores on data the model did not train on.

prediction_cards.label: Repeated data slices
prediction_cards.hint: Same resident stays on one side of the split
prediction_cards.definition: Concern-triage score when the calendar is sliced different ways while keeping each resident in one slice only.

cause_cards.title: History is only earlier sessions
cause_cards.body: Prior counts, prior concern rate, and past mood use recordings before this session’s date.
cause_cards.definition: Future sessions never feed into the history fields for an earlier row.

calls_to_action: Consider a supervisor pass on session 1145 (resident 22) — estimated concern likelihood 77%.
Consider a supervisor pass on session 1135 (resident 22) — estimated concern likelihood 75%.
Consider a supervisor pass on session 1130 (resident 22) — estimated concern likelihood 74%.

emotion_trend_bullets: Patterns tied to mood level at the start of the session (simple score) line up with weaker positive movement (or more negative) on average in past records. That is association only—staff choose interventions for harder cases too.

Patterns tied to staff recording as SW-12 line up with weaker positive movement (or more negative) on average in past records. That is association only—staff choose interventions for harder cases too.

Patterns tied to staff recording as SW-18 line up with weaker positive movement (or more negative) on average in past records. That is association only—staff choose interventions for harder cases too.
Patterns tied to staff recording as SW-16 line up with stronger positive mood movement on average in past records. That is association only—staff choose interventions for harder cases too.
Patterns tied to staff recording as SW-07 line up with stronger positive mood movement on average in past records. That is association only—staff choose interventions for harder cases too.
concern_review_cues: Risk level on file for the resident — in similar past records this tended to line up with a concern flag more often. Use that as a review cue, not proof that it caused the flag.
Residents with more prior counseling visits — in similar past records this tended to line up with a concern flag more often. Use that as a review cue, not proof that it caused the flag.

Residents whose earlier sessions were often concern-flagged — in similar past records this tended to line up with a concern flag more often. Use that as a review cue, not proof that it caused the flag.
Starting mood score for the visit — in similar past records this tended to line up with a concern flag more often. Use that as a review cue, not proof that it caused the flag.
Visits with more interventions listed — in similar past records this tended to line up with a concern flag more often. Use that as a review cue, not proof that it caused the flag.
plain_answers.method_note: Mood change uses a simple start→end score (not a diagnosis). Concern likelihood is a triage aid. Patterns reflect past records, not proof that one intervention caused an outcome.
